title: Dimension Generator
description: Algorithmic member generation with machine-discoverable OpenAPI definitions, applicable to any dimension type. A generator exposes up to five capabilities as endpoints: /generate (paginated member production), /extent (boundary computation), /inverse (value-to-coordinate mapping; requires invertible:true), /search (query-based member discovery), and the optional hierarchical pair /children + /ancestors (requires Hierarchical conformance level). Capabilities are declared in the generator's OpenAPI specification.

type.description: Generator type identifier. Short identifiers name well-known algorithms maintained by the OGC Naming Authority; full URIs identify custom generators whose OpenAPI definition is given in the 'api' field. Well-known types include 'dekadal' (FAO 10-day calendar, 36/year), 'pentadal-monthly' (month-aligned 5-day, 72/year), 'pentadal-annual' (year-aligned 5-day, 73/year), 'integer-range' (evenly-spaced integer bins), 'static-tree' (recursive in-memory hierarchy), and 'leveled-tree' (leveled in-memory hierarchy with ?level=N filtering). This list is extensible: implementations MAY register additional well-known types through the OGC Naming Authority, and custom generators use a full URI as the type value.

api.description: OpenAPI definition URI. REQUIRED when type is a full URI (custom generator). For well-known types the API definition is implicit (resolvable from the registered OGC Definition URI). Machine clients fetch this to discover all available endpoints and parameters.

parameters.description: Default input parameters for the algorithm, defined per JSON Schema 2020-12. Maps to the OpenAPI request schema. For hierarchical generators using a leveled strategy, individual hierarchy levels override these defaults via their own parameters object.

output.description: Type and structure of each generated member, defined per JSON Schema 2020-12. For recursive hierarchical generators, the output object SHOULD include the field named in hierarchy.parent_property, typed as string or null (null for root members).

invertible.description: true if the generator supports inverse operations (Invertible conformance level). Exposes /inverse endpoint: maps a raw value back to the dimension member it belongs to. The forward function is a total surjection from domain values to members, partitioning the domain into disjoint intervals; /inverse computes the unique member containing a given value. Most useful for deterministic generators such as temporal calendars and integer ranges.

search.description: Supported search protocols (Searchable / Similarity conformance levels). 'exact': exact match on member code. 'range': members within bounds. 'like': pattern match (fnmatch-style). 'vector': k-nearest-neighbour search by embedding similarity.

on_invalid.description: Item ingestion behavior when inverse validation fails (invertible generators only). 'reject': return 422, block ingestion. 'accept': allow the item and grow the dimension. 'warn': allow the item and emit a warning. Ignored when invertible is false.

hierarchical.description: true if the generator supports Hierarchical conformance level: /children, /ancestors, and the optional ?parent= filter on /generate. SHOULD be true when the dimension declares a hierarchy property. The generator's /children endpoint follows the same pagination contract as /generate and mirrors the STAC API Children Extension (https://api.stacspec.org/v1.0.0-rc.2/children) applied to dimension members rather than collections.

navigable.description: true if the generator supports per-member navigation links (children, ancestors) when the client requests them via ?links=true. Requires hierarchical:true. When navigable is true and a client passes ?links=true, each member object in the response MAY include a 'links' array with rel:children (present only for non-leaf members) and rel:ancestors entries. By default (?links=false or omitted), member-level links are suppressed to minimize response size. Response-level pagination links (self, next, prev) are always included regardless of this setting.
